name: My NY company inputted privileged docs into Google AI Overview during litigation—did I just waive privilege?
description: Yes, waived per NY Heppner—AI is third party, no protection.[1]

mainEntity.name: My NY company inputted privileged docs into Google AI Overview during litigation—did I just waive privilege?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, waived per NY Heppner—AI is third party, no protection.[1]
acceptedAnswer.description: Yes, per the SDNY Heppner ruling, inputting privileged info into consumer AI like Google AI Overviews waives attorney-client privilege and work product protection due to third-party disclosure via privacy policies.[1][4] Court treated AI as non-confidential third party. Factors: AI terms allow data retention/training, no legal advice purpose, independent client use. Immediately notify counsel, assess disclosure risks, prohibit further AI use. Implement AI governance policies now.
